explanation:
TRUE POSITIVE. The vulnerability is confirmed through direct code analysis of NFT.sol.

Root cause: In `createNFTsFromTokens` (line 219), the check `if (executionDate.isTimeSet2() && cond.date.endTime > executionDate.endTime)` is guarded by `executionDate.isTimeSet2()`. When a sender creates an NFT without specifying execution timestamps (day set, startTime/endTime = 0), `isTimeSet2()` returns false and the condition end-time bound check is entirely skipped.

Exploit path: (1) Alice calls `createNFTsFromTokens` with `executionDate.startTime=0, endTime=0` (triggering WAIT_EXECUTION_DATE status) and a condition with `endTime` set 10 years in the future. (2) The bypass at line 219 allows the malicious condition date to be stored. (3) No tokens are transferred from Alice at this point — `_createNFT` is only called if `nftStatus == CREATED` (line 271), which it isn't. (4) When Bob calls `setExecutionStartTimestamp`, the function computes `maxTime` from stored conditions (10 years out) and `endTimestamp = timestamp + (day+1)*86400` (~31 days). The check `if (maxTime > endTimestamp)` at line 300 reverts, trapping Bob.

Attack cost to attacker: zero (no tokens locked, only allowance checked at creation). Victim has no cancellation path for NFTs in WAIT_EXECUTION_DATE state. The griefing is complete and irreversible.
